data.description:
# Continuous auto-deploy: the running Gerrit environment reflects `main` without manual deploy or restart

## Why
Landing a change on `main` does NOT update the running environment. Gerrit's `refs/meta/config`
(project.config), site `replication.config`, the g2p config, and — critically — the **review-bot
container's code** all require a MANUAL deploy (SSM / setup-*.sh) and sometimes a restart. This
causes drift and toil: e.g. epic 88ab S2's merge-change review code landed on `main` but the
running `compose-review-bot-1` still runs the pre-S2 image, so live merge-review (S5) would test
stale code; the S3 `replication.config` fix had to be hand-applied via SSM. We need the deployed
environment to AUTO-REFLECT `main` shortly after a change lands, with no human on the box.

## What
An automatic update mechanism on the Gerrit host (`i-00880b2c7f13527c5`, the compose stack) that,
when `main` advances (and only for a `main` that passed the two-vote gate + replicated), pulls the
new `main` and idempotently re-applies/reloads the environment:
- **Source sync:** fetch/checkout the new `main` into the box's deploy checkout (the compose build
  context).
- **Config re-apply (idempotent, change-detected):** re-run the declarative appliers only when the
  relevant file changed — `setup-project.sh` (refs/meta/config), `setup-replication.sh`
  (replication.config; `autoReload=true` means no restart), `materialize-g2p-config.sh` (g2p +
  replication bind-mount copy). Each is already declarative/idempotent (a clean re-run is a no-op).
- **Container redeploy (change-detected):** rebuild + restart the `review-bot` service ONLY when its
  sources (src/rebar/**, Dockerfile.reviewbot, pyproject) changed since the deployed SHA
  (`docker compose up -d --build review-bot`); a Gerrit-image change likewise. No-op otherwise.
- **Safety:** deploy ONLY a `main` commit whose CI `Verified` passed (never deploy an unverified
  tip); a health check after redeploy; on failure, do NOT leave a half-updated env (roll back the
  container to the prior image; keep the prior config `.bak`).
- **Trigger:** choose ONE and justify in the plan — (a) a systemd timer on the box polling
  `origin/main` every N minutes + the deploy script, or (b) a GitHub Actions `deploy.yml` on
  `push: main` that invokes the deploy script via SSM. Record the chosen mechanism + cadence/latency.
- **Observability:** structured deploy log + a CloudWatch metric/alarm for deploy failures
  (mirroring the existing host-probe pattern); each deploy records the from→to SHA.
- **Back-out:** a documented way to disable auto-deploy (stop the timer / disable the workflow) and
  the manual deploy path remains available.

## Scope
The deploy orchestrator + its trigger + safety/observability/back-out on the existing compose stack.
Reuses the existing idempotent setup-*.sh appliers (does NOT rewrite them). No change to the gate
semantics (S1/S2) or the feature-branch flow (88ab); this is the deployment substrate they run on.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A change landing on `main` (post-gate + replicated) causes the running environment to reflect
      it within a bounded window (record the latency) with NO manual action on the box
- [ ] Config appliers re-run idempotently and change-detected (a no-op when the file is unchanged;
      re-applied when it changed) — verified for replication.config + refs/meta/config + g2p
- [ ] The review-bot container is rebuilt + restarted ONLY when its sources changed since the
      deployed SHA (verified: a code change to src/rebar/** triggers a redeploy; a docs-only change
      does not), and after redeploy the running bot reflects the new code (proven end-to-end)
- [ ] Safety: only a `Verified`-passing `main` tip is deployed; a post-redeploy health check gates
      success; a failed deploy does not leave a half-updated environment (rollback proven)
- [ ] Chosen trigger mechanism + cadence recorded; deploy log + failure metric/alarm applied
- [ ] Back-out documented + tested (auto-deploy can be disabled; manual path still works)
- [ ] Live-verified end-to-end on the running box (a real main change auto-deploys)
data.title: Continuous auto-deploy: running Gerrit environment reflects main without manual deploy or restart
